content.problemStatement:
Write a program to search for a specific element in an array and print "**Yes**" if the element is present, otherwise print "**No**".


Input Format

The first line contains an integer nnn, the length of the array and kkk, the element to be search.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format

Print "**Yes**" if the element kkk is present in the array.
Print "**No**" if the element kkk is not present in the array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers n and k — the length of the array and the element to search for. The second line contains n space‑separated integers a₁, a₂, …, aₙ.
content.outputFormat: Print "Yes" if k is present in the array, otherwise print "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: The element 1 appears in the array, so the answer is Yes.
The element 4 is not present in the array, therefore the output is No.
The element 5 appears as the last element, so the program prints Yes.

solution.approach: Scan the array from left to right and check each element against k. If any element equals k, the answer is "Yes"; otherwise after the whole scan the answer is "No". This linear scan uses O(1) extra memory and runs in O(n) time, which easily satisfies the constraints.
